    {"repo": "etcd-io/etcd", "path": "client/v3/client.go", "symbol": "dial"},
    {"repo": "etcd-io/etcd", "path": "client/v3/client.go", "symbol": "dialSetupOpts"},
    {"repo": "etcd-io/etcd", "path": "client/v3/client.go", "symbol": "unaryClientInterceptor"},
    {"repo": "etcd-io/etcd", "path": "client/v3/client.go", "symbol": "streamClientInterceptor"},
    {"repo": "etcd-io/etcd", "path": "client/v3/internal/resolver/resolver.go", "symbol": "EtcdManualResolver"},
    {"repo": "etcd-io/etcd", "path": "client/v3/kv.go", "symbol": "NewKV"},
    {"repo": "etcd-io/etcd", "path": "client/v3/retry.go", "symbol": "RetryKVClient"},
    {"repo": "etcd-io/etcd", "path": "client/v3/watch.go", "symbol": "NewWatcher"},
    {"repo": "etcd-io/etcd", "path": "client/v3/lease.go", "symbol": "NewLease"},
    {"repo": "etcd-io/etcd", "path": "client/v3/ctx.go", "symbol": "WithRequireLeader"},
    {"repo": "etcd-io/etcd", "path": "client/v3/utils.go", "symbol": "jitterUp"}
  ],
  "text": "The etcd-io/etcd client implements a comprehensive client-side retry and timeout policy system through several interconnected components:\n\n## gRPC Retry Interceptor\nThe `client/v3/retry_interceptor.go` file contains the core retry logic through `unaryClientInterceptor` and `streamClientInterceptor` functions. These interceptors wrap gRPC calls and implement retry semantics with exponential backoff. The `serverStreamingRetryingStream` type handles stream-specific retry logic by buffering messages and reestablishing connections on failure. Key functions include `isSafeRetry`, `isSafeRetryImmutableRPC`, and `isSafeRetryMutableRPC` which determine whether a request can be safely retried based on the error type and whether it's a read (immutable) or write (mutable) operation.\n\n## Endpoint Balancer\nThe `client/v3/internal/resolver/resolver.go` implements `EtcdManualResolver` which manages endpoint addresses and configures the gRPC resolver with a round-robin load balancing policy. This component dynamically updates endpoint addresses through `SetEndpoints` and ensures balanced request distribution across etcd cluster members using the \"round_robin\" load balancing policy.\n\n## Connection Health Check\nConnection health checking is implemented in `client/v3/client.go` through the `waitForConnection` function, which uses the gRPC health check API (`google.golang.org/grpc/health/grpc_health_v1`) to verify server connectivity. The health check is performed during client initialization with a configurable timeout and handles cases where the health endpoint may not be implemented.\n\n## Request Timeout Enforcement\nTimeout enforcement is distributed across multiple files:\n\n### KV Operations (`client/v3/kv.go`)\nThe KV service wraps the proto client with `RetryKVClient` which applies repeatable (read) and non-repeatable (write) retry policies. The `NewKV` function initializes the KV service with call options from the client configuration, which include timeout settings and message size limits.\n\n### Watch Operations (`client/v3/watch.go`)\nWatch operations support streaming with context-based timeout control. The Watcher interface documents that context cancellation or timeout results in closed watch channels. The watch implementation uses the provided context for both the stream lifetime and individual message timeouts.\n\n### Lease Operations (`client/v3/lease.go`)\nLease operations through the lessor implement timeout management through:\n- `firstKeepAliveTimeout`: A timeout for initial keep-alive requests (defaults to 5 seconds or configured DialTimeout)\n- Context-based timeouts for individual lease operations like `Grant`, `Revoke`, `TimeToLive`, and `Leases`\n- The KeepAlive streaming operation maintains deadline tracking in the `keepAlive` structure for each lease\n\n## Configuration and Timeout Settings\nThe `client/v3/config.go` defines timeout-related configuration:\n- `DialTimeout`: Connection establishment timeout\n- `DialKeepAliveTime`: Keep-alive ping interval\n- `DialKeepAliveTimeout`: Keep-alive response timeout\n- `MaxUnaryRetries`: Maximum retry count (defaults to 100)\n- `BackoffWaitBetween`: Wait time before retry (defaults to 25ms)\n- `BackoffJitterFraction`: Jitter randomization (defaults to 0.10)\n\nThe `client/v3/options.go` file defines default call options including `defaultWaitForReady` (true), message size limits, and retry parameters.\n\n## Context and Error Handling\nThe `client/v3/ctx.go` provides context utilities like `WithRequireLeader` for enforcing leader requirements. Context error handling is centralized in `ContextError` and `IsConnCanceled` functions in `client/v3/client.go`, which properly distinguish between context-level timeouts/cancellations and gRPC-level errors.\n\n## Backoff and Jitter\nThe `client/v3/utils.go` implements the `jitterUp` function for randomized exponential backoff. The `client/v3/client.go` implements `roundRobinQuorumBackoff` which applies backoff after each round of retry attempts across quorum members, optimizing retry patterns for cluster scenarios.\n\n## Endpoint Translation\nThe `client/v3/internal/endpoint/endpoint.go` handles endpoint URL parsing and credential requirement determination, supporting various schemes (http, https, unix, unixs) and translating them to gRPC-compatible addresses.\n\n## Summary\nThe retry and timeout policy forms a multi-layered system: configuration establishes timeouts and retry limits, the resolver manages endpoint balancing, interceptors apply retry logic with backoff, context controls timeout enforcement at the operation level, and the health check ensures connection validity. Write operations use stricter retry policies (non-repeatable) to maintain at-most-once semantics, while read operations use repeatable policies for safety."
}

conversation_events.text:
# etcd Client gRPC Retry and Timeout Policy

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in etcd-io/etcd that implement client-side retry and timeout policy: the gRPC retry interceptor, the endpoint balancer, the connection health check, and the request timeout enforcement for KV, Watch, and Lease operations.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the org domain.

## Available Resources

No local repositories are pre-checked out.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
*(none — all repos available locally)*

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find the Go source files in etcd-io/etcd that implement client-side retry and timeout policy. Let me start by checking if we have a local repository and then systematically search for the relevant components.
Now let me search for a few more related files to ensure comprehensive coverage.
Now let me create the answer.json file with all my findings.

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive analysis of the etcd-io/etcd codebase and identified all the Go source files implementing client-side retry and timeout policy. Here's what I found:

## Summary of Findings

I've created `/workspace/answer.json` with detailed findings covering:

### **1. gRPC Retry Interceptor** (client/v3/retry_interceptor.go)
- `unaryClientInterceptor`: Handles retry logic for unary (non-streaming) calls
- `streamClientInterceptor`: Manages retry for server-streaming calls
- `serverStreamingRetryingStream`: Buffers and replays messages on retry
- `isSafeRetry`: Determines retry safety based on error type and request type

### **2. Endpoint Balancer** (client/v3/internal/resolver/resolver.go)
- `EtcdManualResolver`: Manages multiple endpoint addresses
- Implements round-robin load balancing across cluster members
- Dynamically updates endpoints via `SetEndpoints()`

### **3. Connection Health Check** (client/v3/client.go)
- `waitForConnection()`: Uses gRPC health check API during dial
- Validates server connectivity before returning connection
- Handles cases where health endpoint is unimplemented

### **4. Request Timeout Enforcement**
- **KV operations** (client/v3/kv.go): Wraps with `RetryKVClient` for safe retries
- **Watch operations** (client/v3/watch.go): Context-based timeout control
- **Lease operations** (client/v3/lease.go): `firstKeepAliveTimeout`, deadline tracking, context management
- **Transactions** (client/v3/txn.go): Context timeout propagation

### **5. Configuration & Utilities**
- Timeout settings: `DialTimeout`, `DialKeepAliveTime`, `DialKeepAliveTimeout`
- Retry parameters: `MaxUnaryRetries` (default 100), backoff wait (25ms), jitter (0.10)
- `jitterUp()`: Randomized exponential backoff implementation

The analysis includes 17 files, 30+ key symbols, and a detailed call chain showing how these components interact to provide robust client-side resilience.

  "text": "The etcd-io/etcd client implements a comprehensive client-side retry and timeout policy system through several interconnected components:\n\n## gRPC Retry Interceptor\nThe `client/v3/retry_interceptor.go` file contains the core retry logic through `unaryClientInterceptor` and `streamClientInterceptor` functions. These interceptors wrap gRPC calls and implement retry semantics with exponential backoff. The `serverStreamingRetryingStream` type handles stream-specific retry logic by buffering messages and reestablishing connections on failure. Key functions include `isSafeRetry`, `isSafeRetryImmutableRPC`, and `isSafeRetryMutableRPC` which determine whether a request can be safely retried based on the error type and whether it's a read (immutable) or write (mutable) operation.\n\n## Endpoint Balancer\nThe `client/v3/internal/resolver/resolver.go` implements `EtcdManualResolver` which manages endpoint addresses and configures the gRPC resolver with a round-robin load balancing policy. This component dynamically updates endpoint addresses through `SetEndpoints` and ensures balanced request distribution across etcd cluster members using the \"round_robin\" load balancing policy.\n\n## Connection Health Check\nConnection health checking is implemented in `client/v3/client.go` through the `waitForConnection` function, which uses the gRPC health check API (`google.golang.org/grpc/health/grpc_health_v1`) to verify server connectivity. The health check is performed during client initialization with a configurable timeout and handles cases where the health endpoint may not be implemented.\n\n## Request Timeout Enforcement\nTimeout enforcement is distributed across multiple files:\n\n### KV Operations (`client/v3/kv.go`)\nThe KV service wraps the proto client with `RetryKVClient` which applies repeatable (read) and non-repeatable (write) retry policies. The `NewKV` function initializes the KV service with call options from the client configuration, which include timeout settings and message size limits.\n\n### Watch Operations (`client/v3/watch.go`)\nWatch operations support streaming with context-based timeout control. The Watcher interface documents that context cancellation or timeout results in closed watch channels. The watch implementation uses the provided context for both the stream lifetime and individual message timeouts.\n\n### Lease Operations (`client/v3/lease.go`)\nLease operations through the lessor implement timeout management through:\n- `firstKeepAliveTimeout`: A timeout for initial keep-alive requests (defaults to 5 seconds or configured DialTimeout)\n- Context-based timeouts for individual lease operations like `Grant`, `Revoke`, `TimeToLive`, and `Leases`\n- The KeepAlive streaming operation maintains deadline tracking in the `keepAlive` structure for each lease\n\n## Configuration and Timeout Settings\nThe `client/v3/config.go` defines timeout-related configuration:\n- `DialTimeout`: Connection establishment timeout\n- `DialKeepAliveTime`: Keep-alive ping interval\n- `DialKeepAliveTimeout`: Keep-alive response timeout\n- `MaxUnaryRetries`: Maximum retry count (defaults to 100)\n- `BackoffWaitBetween`: Wait time before retry (defaults to 25ms)\n- `BackoffJitterFraction`: Jitter randomization (defaults to 0.10)\n\nThe `client/v3/options.go` file defines default call options including `defaultWaitForReady` (true), message size limits, and retry parameters.\n\n## Context and Error Handling\nThe `client/v3/ctx.go` provides context utilities like `WithRequireLeader` for enforcing leader requirements. Context error handling is centralized in `ContextError` and `IsConnCanceled` functions in `client/v3/client.go`, which properly distinguish between context-level timeouts/cancellations and gRPC-level errors.\n\n## Backoff and Jitter\nThe `client/v3/utils.go` implements the `jitterUp` function for randomized exponential backoff. The `client/v3/client.go` implements `roundRobinQuorumBackoff` which applies backoff after each round of retry attempts across quorum members, optimizing retry patterns for cluster scenarios.\n\n## Endpoint Translation\nThe `client/v3/internal/endpoint/endpoint.go` handles endpoint URL parsing and credential requirement determination, supporting various schemes (http, https, unix, unixs) and translating them to gRPC-compatible addresses.\n\n## Summary\nThe retry and timeout policy forms a multi-layered system: configuration establishes timeouts and retry limits, the resolver manages endpoint balancing, interceptors apply retry logic with backoff, context controls timeout enforcement at the operation level, and the health check ensures connection validity. Write operations use stricter retry policies (non-repeatable) to maintain at-most-once semantics, while read operations use repeatable policies for safety."
}
